events.label: assistant
events.text: Assessment written to `/app/answer.json`: this is a **fail**, critical risk. Missing a stored energy source (hydraulic/gravity/capacitor) during LOTO violates OSHA 1910.147(d)(5)/(c)(4)(ii)(B), which mandate that all hazardous energy — including residual/stored energy — be identified, blocked/bled, and verified at zero-energy state, not just the primary electrical/mechanical supply. This is a well-known root cause of fatal LOTO incidents since workers reasonably assume the equipment is safe once visible lockout devices are applied. Corrective actions include blocking/bleeding the stored energy, revising the machine-specific procedure, retraining, and re-verifying zero energy state before closeout.
